summary.name: Systematic Short SPY Put Premium Harvesting
summary.author: player two
summary.description: The strategy systematically sells short-dated SPY put options to harvest option risk premium, targeting the persistent supply demand imbalance in equity index downside protection. Each trading day, 30 minutes after the SPY cash open, it scans the listed SPY option chain and selects a put with 40 to 55 days to expiry, positive bid, and nonzero volume, then chooses the furthest expiry in that window and within it the highest strike (i.e., the most at-the-money to in-the-money put available), maxim
summary.description_full:
The strategy systematically sells short-dated SPY put options to harvest option risk premium, targeting the persistent supply demand imbalance in equity index downside protection. Each trading day, 30 minutes after the SPY cash open, it scans the listed SPY option chain and selects a put with 40 to 55 days to expiry, positive bid, and nonzero volume, then chooses the furthest expiry in that window and within it the highest strike (i.e., the most at-the-money to in-the-money put available), maximizing premium and delta exposure versus farther out-of-the-money structures.

Position sizing is governed by margin based limits rather than volatility or delta: the algorithm sells as many contracts as allowed such that the notional strike exposure uses no more than 20 percent of available buying power for that trade, and it will not place the order if doing so would push portfolio margin utilization above 50 percent. There is no discretionary exit; positions persist until expiration or assignment. If assigned, the resulting long SPY shares are immediately liquidated, keeping the portfolio structurally short puts rather than long equity. Decisions are made once per day using live option quotes and volume filters; the intended edge is systematic collection of equity index put premium, with explicit controls to cap leverage and assignment induced equity exposure duration.

Position sizing is governed by margin based limits rather than volatility or delta: the algorithm sells as many contracts as allowed such that the notional strike exposure uses no more than 20 percent of available buying power for that trade, and it will not place the order if doing so would push portfolio margin utilization above 50 percent. Decisions are made once per day using live option quotes and volume filters; the intended edge is systematic collection of equity index put premium, with explicit controls to cap leverage and assignment induced equity exposure duration.
